requirements_for_qualification.summary: Must donate long-term appreciated stocks, bonds, or mutual funds directly to qualified charity to receive fair market value deduction while avoiding capital gains tax on appreciation, providing dual tax benefit
criteria: Securities must be held over 12 months for long-term capital gain treatment and full FMV deduction
Donate directly to qualified 501(c)(3) public charity (not donor-advised fund for full FMV treatment)
Deduction based on fair market value on date of transfer, not original cost basis
Avoids capital gains tax on built-in appreciation that would be owed if sold first then donated cash
Contribution deduction limited to 30% of AGI for appreciated securities (vs 60% for cash donations)
Excess contributions carry forward 5 years retaining character as appreciated property donations
Must obtain qualified appraisal for donations over $5,000 and file Form 8283
Donor must not receive any goods or services in exchange for contribution

appreciated_securities_strategy.dual_benefit: Charitable deduction at FMV + avoid capital gains tax on appreciation = enhanced tax benefit
appreciated_securities_strategy.deduction_calculation: Deduct full fair market value if held over 12 months and donate to public charity
appreciated_securities_strategy.capital_gains_avoided: Never pay 15-20% long-term capital gains tax (plus 3.8% NIIT) on appreciation
appreciated_securities_strategy.agi_limit: 30% of AGI annual limit for appreciated property; 5-year carryforward for excess
appreciated_securities_strategy.optimal_candidates: Low-basis highly-appreciated holdings ideal; maximize appreciation vs basis ratio
appreciated_securities_strategy.portfolio_rebalancing: Donate appreciated position and repurchase if want to maintain allocation

restrictions: Must hold securities over 12 months; short-term capital gain property limited to lesser of FMV or basis
30% AGI limitation more restrictive than 60% limit for cash contributions
Private foundations: deduction limited to basis (not FMV) for appreciated property donations
Donor-advised funds: same 30% AGI limit but provides timing flexibility for grants to charities
Cannot receive any benefit in return; reduces deduction by value of benefits received
Related-use rule for tangible personal property: use unrelated to charity's purpose limits deduction to basis
Must obtain contemporaneous written acknowledgment from charity for donations over $250
Form 8283 required for non-cash contributions over $500; qualified appraisal required over $5,000
